description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a rectangular, boxy form and a woodgrain exterior finish. The front features a convex glass screen bordered by a thick, dark bezel, with two large rotary dials and several push buttons below the screen, all set in a metallic panel. The sides and top are covered in a reddish-brown wood veneer, giving it a classic mid-20th-century appearance. The back panel is metallic with a perforated pattern for ventilation and a large protruding cylindrical component, likely for the cathode ray tube, along with several smaller knobs and ports. The television stands on four small feet, slightly elevating it from the surface. The overall design is robust and utilitarian, with visible wear and scratches that suggest age and frequent use.
description: A vintage, wood-veneered television with a convex glass screen, metallic control panel, and a perforated metal back with a large cylindrical protrusion.
description_view_0: Side view showing the reddish-brown woodgrain finish and a large black knob protruding from the back.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the convex glass screen, thick bezel, two rotary dials, and several push buttons below the screen.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, with the woodgrain finish and the cylindrical back component visible.
description_view_3: Rear view highlighting the perforated metal back panel, large cylindrical tube, and several smaller knobs and ports.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick, dark bezel with metallic trim, matte finish, and rounded corners.
attribute_control_button_layout: Two large rotary dials and several push buttons arranged in a row below the screen on the front panel.
